A defendant may receive a harsher sentence after retrial only when justified by objective events occurring after the original sentence.

sections.title: 169. Collateral Estoppel
body: The government may not relitigate an ultimate factual issue necessarily resolved in the defendant’s favor by a valid acquittal.
The defendant bears the burden to show the issue was necessarily decided.

blocks.text: The government may not relitigate an ultimate factual issue necessarily resolved in the defendant’s favor by a valid acquittal.
The defendant bears the burden to show the issue was necessarily decided.

sections.title: 170. Appeals
body: The Constitution does not independently guarantee an appeal.
When a first appeal as of right exists, an indigent defendant is entitled to counsel and equal access.
There is no constitutional right to self-representation on appeal.

blocks.text: The Constitution does not independently guarantee an appeal.
When a first appeal as of right exists, an indigent defendant is entitled to counsel and equal access.
There is no constitutional right to self-representation on appeal.

sections.title: 171. Harmless and Plain Error
body: Harmless Error
A preserved constitutional error does not require reversal if the government proves beyond a reasonable doubt that it did not contribute to the verdict.
Plain Error
An unpreserved error may justify relief when it is:
Error
Plain
Affects substantial rights
Seriously affects fairness, integrity, or reputation of proceedings
Some structural errors require automatic reversal.

sections.title: 172. Habeas Corpus
body: Habeas corpus is a civil collateral attack on unlawful custody.
The petitioner may satisfy custody while:
In prison
On probation
On parole
An expired sentence generally does not satisfy custody merely because it later enhances another sentence.

blocks.text: Habeas corpus is a civil collateral attack on unlawful custody.

On probation
On parole

sections.title: 173. AEDPA Limits
body: A state prisoner seeking federal habeas relief generally must:
Exhaust state remedies
Overcome deferential review of state-court decisions
Rely primarily on the state-court record
Satisfy procedural restrictions on successive petitions
Relief requires more than showing ordinary legal error.

blocks.text: A state prisoner seeking federal habeas relief generally must:

Rely primarily on the state-court record
Satisfy procedural restrictions on successive petitions
Relief requires more than showing ordinary legal error.

sections.title: 174. Parole and Probation Revocation
body: Due process generally requires:
Notice
Preliminary probable-cause determination
Final hearing
Opportunity to present evidence
Limited confrontation rights
Neutral decisionmaker
Counsel is required when necessary for fundamental fairness or when a new or deferred sentence may be imposed.

sections.title: 182. Final MBE Traps
body: Fourth Amendment rights are personal.
Private action is not government action unless police direct or adopt it.
A show of authority requires submission.
An arrest warrant does not authorize searching a third party’s home.
The home and curtilage receive the greatest protection.
Open fields and abandoned property receive no Fourth Amendment protection.
A warrant must be particular on its face or properly incorporate an accompanying document.
A premises warrant does not automatically authorize searching everyone present.
Search incident to arrest does not permit searching cell-phone data.
Vehicle search incident to arrest requires access or evidence of the arrest offense.
The automobile exception does not authorize entering curtilage.
Terry stop and Terry frisk require separate reasonable suspicions.
A dog sniff cannot prolong a completed traffic stop.
Consent must be voluntary, and scope matters.
A present occupant’s objection defeats another present occupant’s consent.
Miranda requires custody and interrogation.
Miranda counsel must be requested unambiguously.
Physical fruits of unwarned voluntary statements are generally admissible.
The Sixth Amendment is offense specific.
There is no counsel right at a photo array.
Brady includes impeachment evidence.
Jury verdicts must be unanimous.
Testimonial hearsay requires unavailability and prior cross-examination.
Conspiracy and its substantive offense are separate for double jeopardy.
Reversal for insufficient evidence bars retrial.

sections.title: Final One-Sentence Summary
body: Criminal Procedure asks whether the government respected the defendant’s constitutional protections at investigation, charging, trial, sentencing, and review—and whether any violation requires suppression, reversal, or no remedy.
